creator: swell.k@gmail.com
text:
Christian Weisgerber <naddy@FreeBSD.org> writes:

> swell.k@gmail.com:
>
>> > Solved differently (CONFIGURE_TARGET).  Thanks for your report.
>> 
>> I disagree. Please, fix this at the autoconf level, too.
>
> This problem is specifically caused by our ports framework overriding
> the configure target as amd64-portbld-freebsd*. On its own, the
> configure script detects FreeBSD/amd64 as x86_64-unknown-freebsd*
> and automatically enables the corresponding optimizations.

I think libtool is affected, too. Try running this in ${WKRSRC}

    $ libtoolize -if
    $ ./configure

    XZ Utils 4.999.9beta

    System type:
    checking build system type... amd64-unknown-freebsd9.0
    checking host system type... amd64-unknown-freebsd9.0

Imagine when someone creates xz-utils snapshot on FreeBSD that doesn't
work correctly on same platform.
